content:
I'm analyzing the architecture of the Awesome-Researcher-Agent project at /Users/wjx/code/Awesome-Researcher-Agent. I need to understand the core business logic, data flow, and design patterns used.

GOAL: Understand how the agent works internally - what are the core abstractions, how data flows through the system, what design patterns are employed.

Find:
1. Core agent/workflow classes and their relationships (inheritance, composition)
2. How research tasks are defined, executed, and results collected
3. LLM integration patterns (which models, how prompts are structured, tool use)
4. Data models and schemas (Pydantic models, dataclasses, type definitions)
5. State management patterns (how agent state is tracked and persisted)
6. Error handling and retry patterns
7. Any workflow orchestration (DAG, pipeline, chain-of-thought, etc.)
8. Tool/function calling patterns if present

Focus on src/ or the main source directory. Read the most important source files to understand the core abstractions. Return file paths with descriptions of the patterns found.
<!-- OMO_INTERNAL_INITIATOR -->
